subject: Continuous Random Variables
question: Let $F: \mathbb{R} \to \mathbb{R}$ be a function defined by $F(x) = \begin{cases} 0 & x < 0 \\ x/2 & 0 \le x < 1 \\ (x+2)/6 & 1 \le x < 4 \\ 1 & x \ge 4 \end{cases}$ (a) Show that $F$ satisfies conditions (a), (b), (c), and (e) of Theorem 30.1. (b) Find the probability density function $f(x)$.
explanation: Answer: (b) $f(x) = F'(x) = \begin{cases} 0 & x < 0 \\ 1/2 & 0 \le x < 1 \\ 1/6 & 1 \le x < 4 \\ 0 & x \ge 4 \end{cases}$
